title: Star Wars: Jedi Apprentice
content:

'''''Star Wars: Jedi Apprentice''''' is a series of young-reader novels written by [[Jude Watson]] (except for the first book, which was written by [[Dave Wolverton]]) that chronicle [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi's]] exploits prior to [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. They are published by [[Scholastic]]. The back of each volume bears the inscription "Peace over anger. Honor over hate. Strength over fear" above the story's synopsis.

[[Jedi Apprentice: The Rising Force|''The Rising Force'']] and [[Jedi Apprentice: The Dark Rival|''The Dark Rival'']] concern Qui-Gon taking Obi-Wan as a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] and introduce the series' main villain: [[Xanatos]]. [[Jedi Apprentice: The Hidden Past|''The Hidden Past'']] and [[Jedi Apprentice: The Mark of the Crown|''The Mark of the Crown'']] appertain to Obi-Wan and Qui-Gon's further missions in the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]]. [[Jedi Apprentice: The Defenders of the Dead|''The Defenders of the Dead'']] and [[Jedi Apprentice: The Uncertain Path|''The Uncertain Path'']] are about restoring peace to the war-torn [[Melida/Daan/Legends|Melida/Daan]], a mission during which Obi-Wan temporarily leaves the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. In [[Jedi Apprentice: The Captive Temple|''The Captive Temple'']] and [[Jedi Apprentice: The Day of Reckoning|''The Day of Reckoning'']], Obi-Wan and Qui-Gon fight a final battle against Xanatos.

[[Jedi Apprentice: The Fight for Truth|''The Fight for Truth'']] and [[Jedi Apprentice: The Shattered Peace|''The Shattered Peace'']] detail missions in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. Books [[Jedi Apprentice: The Deadly Hunter|11]], [[Jedi Apprentice: The Evil Experiment|12]], and [[Jedi Apprentice: The Dangerous Rescue|13]] form their own story arc with [[Jenna Zan Arbor]] as the antagonist. Books [[Jedi Apprentice: The Ties That Bind|14]]–[[Jedi Apprentice: The Call to Vengeance|16]] concern Obi-Wan and Qui-Gon's mission to help Tahl on the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] planet of [[New Apsolon/Legends|New Apsolon]]. Books [[Jedi Apprentice: The Only Witness|17]] and [[Jedi Apprentice: The Threat Within|18]] detail missions in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]] and the Outer Rim, respectively.

==Continuity==
''The Jedi Apprentice'' series of young reader novels ultimately comprised twenty instalments covering approximately five [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan]] and [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon’s]] relationship. There were still plenty of unexplored time in Obi-Wan’s apprenticeship to allow for further adventures in this era to be told. [[Star Wars: The Clone Wars|''The Clone Wars'' animated series]] ([[2010]]) revealed that Obi-Wan and Qui-Gon once spent a year on [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], a lengthy event not chronicled in these books.<ref name="TERC" />
